purpose: Test hypothesis from §6.11.2 diagnosis: deterministic chain init at the prior mean (vs random init) fixes the warmup-divergence problem. HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED — see comparison below.
init_strategy: init_to_value at prior_log_mean (= cohort hierarchical posterior mean)

comparison_to_random_init_runs.verdict: Deterministic init at the prior mean DRAMATICALLY improves NUTS convergence. Chains now stay in physically reasonable θ region near the hier prior. R-hat is still not clinical-grade (some chain-to-chain variance remains in unidentifiable directions), but the failure mode of 'wandering into nonphysical regions during warmup' is RESOLVED.

posterior_alignment_with_priors.r_T+_alignment: matches hier prior (5× higher than canonical Zhang)
posterior_alignment_with_priors.alpha_T_minus_T_plus_alignment: matches hier prior (50% higher than canonical Zhang) — INDEPENDENT CONFIRMATION of the cohort fit finding from §6.11
posterior_alignment_with_priors.K_TP_drop_alignment: essentially canonical Zhang (well-identified, prior had σ=0.01 in log space)

remaining_work: R-hat = 222 on r_T- and 80 on α(T-,TP) — chains still find different modes in unidentifiable directions
Try: longer warmup (1000-2000) to allow mass-matrix adaptation to settle
Try: target_accept = 0.95 or 0.99 for smaller step sizes in unidentifiable directions
Try: explicit mass-matrix init from FIM-inverse at the prior mean
These are incremental improvements; the fundamental warmup-divergence failure mode is resolved
